verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a type I integral membrane protein resident to the trans-Golgi network (TGN) with documented cycling between early endosomes and TGN. This is an endomembrane compartment protein with no evidence of stable outer-leaflet plasma-membrane residence, ectodomain shedding, cancer-cell ecto-display, cell-death-induced externalization, or dual localization to the PM. The protein cycles within the secretory pathway but does not establish a surface-accessible pool under baseline or non-baseline conditions.
